prevalence.trend: Stable with fluctuations due to seasonal influenza and COVID-19 surges; post-pandemic increase in secondary bacterial pneumonia cases

incidence.source: ECDC Surveillance Reports and WHO European Regional Office data
incidence.notes: Includes community-acquired and hospital-acquired cases; higher during influenza and COVID-19 seasons

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2021 (IHME) adapted for EU; ECDC morbidity reports

vulnerablePopulations: Those with comorbidities during flu/COVID-19 seasons
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Combination of clinical symptoms (fever, productive cough, dyspnea, chest pain), physical exam findings (tachypnea, crackles), imaging (chest X-ray or CT showing consolidation/infiltrates), and microbiological confirmation (sputum/blood cultures, urinary antigens for Streptococcus pneumoniae or Legionella); guided by ERS/ATS criteria with considerations for atypical presentations in COVID-19 co-infections

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: No routine population screening; pneumococcal vaccination coverage ~60-70% in adults >65 years and children, varying by member state (e.g., higher in Nordic countries)
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: ~900,000 new cases annually (including ~200,000 secondary to influenza/COVID-19)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 70-80% effective treatment with antibiotics; vaccination prevents ~50% of invasive pneumococcal disease; antimicrobial resistance control via EU-wide stewardship programs

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Harmonized across EU per European Respiratory Society (ERS) guidelines, with rapid PCR testing for COVID-19/influenza co-infections emphasized post-2020
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Empiric antibiotics (e.g., amoxicillin or macrolides for community-acquired; broader for hospitalized cases) per national adaptations of ECDC protocols; supportive care including oxygen/ventilation; antivirals for influenza/COVID-19 associated cases
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Antimicrobial resistance (e.g., multidrug-resistant S. pneumoniae in Southern/Eastern EU); aging population increasing burden; disparities in healthcare access between Western and Eastern member states; seasonal overload from flu/COVID-19 waves
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust surveillance via ECDC's European Surveillance System (TESSy); high vaccination uptake in programs like PCV13/PPV23; advanced ICU capabilities reducing mortality; EU-funded research on post-COVID pneumonia sequelae
